meaning: n.椴歌剛,鍝常v.鍙堝摥鍙堥椆
examples: Leatherbacks do not have blubber, but they do have a reptilian equivalent: thick, oil-saturated skin, with a layer of fibrous, fatty tissue just beneath it.
She started to blubber like a child.
The baby whale develops a thick layer of blubber to protect it from the cold sea.
The proper human diet requires not cuts in bone or muscle but in the blubber.
Called whale-fall, the layers of blubber, internal organs and bones can provide sustenance for many years.
